assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: Mark, Docklane has 15+ engineers in AM/GE/KZ and payment delays have already cost you time twice. Mellow takes the manual escalations off your plate: auto-payments, statuses without chat threads, a named owner when things break. Plus I can give you material for finance if you need to move it internally. What takes the most time in payment coordination right now?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: Specifically what stops being manual: payment status follow-ups, exception handling, and reconciliation. Mellow owns the payment workflow, KYC checks, contractor documents, and incident handling on our side, while auto-payments and real-time statuses replace the chat threads and spreadsheet updates.
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

transcript.text: Mark, Docklane has 15+ engineers in AM/GE/KZ and payment delays have already cost you time twice. Mellow takes the manual escalations off your plate: auto-payments, statuses without chat threads, a named owner when things break. Plus I can give you material for finance if you need to move it internally. What takes the most time in payment coordination right now?
Ok, you spotted the pain. But I already had one vendor who promised the same.

Specifically what stops being manual: payment status follow-ups, exception handling, and reconciliation. Mellow owns the payment workflow, KYC checks, contractor documents, and incident handling on our side, while auto-payments and real-time statuses replace the chat threads and spreadsheet updates.
Too early. You haven't convinced me yet this isn't just another vendor.

Specifically what stops being manual: payment status follow-ups, exception handling, and reconciliation. Mellow owns the payment workflow, KYC checks, contractor documents, and incident handling on our side, while auto-payments and real-time statuses replace the chat threads and spreadsheet updates. Which part of your process is eating the most time right now?
Fine. Send flow and specifically what escalations get handled automatically.

Concretely: Mellow owns contractor documents, KYC checks, the payment workflow, and incident handling on our side. Auto-payments remove the chase, real-time statuses remove the follow-up thread, and your team stops acting as the escalation bridge.
From your current setup, what is creating the most manual work right now?
This sounds like every other vendor pitch. What's concretely different — not in words, but in the mechanism?
